name: Lot
shortDescription: Lot is a figure in the Hebrew Bible, known for his flight from Sodom and Gomorrah and his subsequent actions.
description: Lot is a prominent figure in the Book of Genesis, nephew of Abraham, and known primarily for his association with the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah. He separates from Abraham to settle in the fertile Jordan Valley, eventually residing in Sodom. Despite the wickedness of the city's inhabitants, Lot is considered righteous, as evidenced by God's willingness to spare him and his family from the impending destruction. Warned by angels of God's plan to destroy the cities due to their wickedness, Lot flees Sodom with his wife and two daughters. They are instructed not to look back, but Lot's wife disobeys and is turned into a pillar of salt. After the destruction, Lot and his daughters take refuge in a cave. In a controversial episode, Lot's daughters, believing they are the only people left on Earth, conspire to get their father drunk and have children with him to continue the family line. These unions result in the birth of Moab and Ben-Ammi, progenitors of the Moabites and Ammonites, respectively. Lot's story serves as a cautionary tale about the consequences of living in wicked societies and the importance of obedience to divine commands. His narrative is also interpreted as an explanation for the origins of the Moabite and Ammonite nations. The story of Lot is significant in Jewish tradition, highlighting themes of righteousness, divine judgment, and the complex relationship between humans and God. His character is often debated, with some emphasizing his righteousness and others focusing on the problematic aspects of his story, particularly the incident with his daughters. Lot's association with Abraham also reinforces the importance of family lineage and the covenant between God and Abraham.

sources.title: The Jewish Study Bible
sources.author: Adele Berlin and Marc Zvi Brettler

sources.title: Ancient Israel: From Abraham to the Roman Destruction of the Temple
sources.author: Hershel Shanks
